executor_handoff.system_instructions: Execute one path only. Three independent edits â€” preserve existing behavior exactly. Swift 6.3 strict concurrency. NEVER add `@unchecked Sendable` or `nonisolated(unsafe)`. Out of scope â€” do NOT touch these 4 lock-backed sites (skill-sanctioned, leave verbatim): MetricsCapturingDelegate, ManualClock (the outer class keeps its `@unchecked Sendable`), OutputRecorder, ScriptedStreamFetcher. Dependency: this handoff's TEST-target edits (IDBox, GatedFetcher) can only be build-validated AFTER handoff 001 (the 17-error compile fix) lands. The FindingsLog edit is production and validates independently. Recommend: apply after 001 is green.

task_brief.objective: Remove the 2 skill-unsanctioned `@unchecked Sendable` uses (FindingsLog, IDBox) and upgrade the 1 sanctioned site where an actor is clearer (GatedFetcher). Net effect: 7 `@unchecked Sendable` -> 4, all remaining ones genuine lock-backed/provably-safe.
task_brief.why: swift-concurrency-pro: `@unchecked Sendable` legitimate ONLY for provably-thread-safe lock-backed types. FindingsLog has NO lock (the claim is false) yet is actor-confined so it needs no Sendable at all. IDBox is a bare mutable box leaning on an external lock (smell). GatedFetcher's lock is gratuitous now that `fetch` is async â€” an actor is clearer and drops the cryptic `withLock { defer }` one-liner.
task_brief.repo_signals: - FindingsLog (Sources/ValistreamCore/Archive/FindingsLog.swift): `public final class FindingsLog: @unchecked Sendable`, holds `private let fileHandle: FileHandle`, `append()` writes with no lock. Used ONLY as `private var findingsLog` on `ValidationSession` (a `public actor`): constructed in `ValidationSession.run()` (~line 168), appended in the finding-recording path (~line 397). Plus single-threaded `FindingsLogTests`. Never sent across an isolation boundary. - IDBox: `private final class IDBox: @unchecked Sendable { var id = -1 }` nested in ManualClock (Tests/ValistreamTests/Support/ManualClock.swift). Used only inside `ManualClock.sleep(until:tolerance:)` to carry a sleeper id from the continuation body into the `onCancel` handler. Both accesses already happen under `ManualClock.lock`. - GatedFetcher: `final class GatedFetcher: StreamFetching, @unchecked Sendable` in Tests/ValistreamTests/OneShotInterruptTests.swift. `let inner/gateURL/gate` + one lock-guarded `hasGated` Bool. `fetch(_:) async -> FetchResult` is the only API.
task_brief.scope: Exactly 3 edits: FindingsLog, IDBox, GatedFetcher. Nothing else. The 4 KEEP sites stay byte-for-byte. No production change beyond the FindingsLog declaration line.
task_brief.code_level_guidance: - FindingsLog: only references are ValidationSession (actor `private var`) + single-threaded tests; nothing sends it. Dropping Sendable is safe and produces no new error. - IDBox elimination: id is now allocated even on the immediate-resume path (harmless nextID bump). The deadline is still re-checked under the second lock acquisition. onCancel finds nothing when the sleeper was never appended -> no-op. Behavior is identical to the box version. - GatedFetcher reentrancy: `hasGated = true` is set BEFORE the `await gate.block()` suspension, with no await between the `!hasGated` read and the write -> the gate still fires exactly once even under concurrent gateURL fetches. `fetch` was already async, so NO call site changes. It remains Sendable (actor) -> still satisfies `StreamFetching: Sendable`. - Forbidden: editing the 4 KEEP sites; ManualClock's OUTER `@unchecked Sendable` stays (only the nested IDBox is removed); any new `@unchecked`/`nonisolated(unsafe)`.
task_brief.search_fallback: grep -rn "@unchecked Sendable" sources/Valistream --include=*.swift After this handoff it must return EXACTLY 4 lines: MetricsCapturingDelegate, ManualClock, OutputRecorder, ScriptedStreamFetcher. `IDBox` type must be gone.
task_brief.acceptance_criteria: - `grep -rn "@unchecked Sendable"` over the package = exactly 4 (the KEEP set). No `IDBox` type. - No new `@unchecked Sendable` / `nonisolated(unsafe)`. - Production builds clean. After 001: test target builds; tests pass with unchanged behavior â€” esp. OneShotInterruptTests (one-shot interrupt still gates exactly once), FindingsLogTests, and ManualClock-driven cadence suites (sleep/cancel still deterministic).
task_brief.validation: Prod (independent): swift build --package-path sources/Valistream 2>&1 | xcsift -f toon Test (needs 001): swift build --build-tests --package-path sources/Valistream 2>&1 | xcsift -f toon swift test --package-path sources/Valistream 2>&1 | xcsift -f toon Then `Valistream-Package` scheme build + test green. Targeted: run OneShotInterruptTests + FindingsLogTests + the ManualClock cancellation paths; confirm no behavioral drift from the box removal or the actor conversion.
